headline: Synopsys (SNPS) Valuation Check As Shares Stall And Ansys Deal Reshapes Growth Story
summary: Why Synopsys stock is drawing investor attention now Synopsys (SNPS) is back on many investors’ watchlists after a mixed stretch, with the share price roughly flat over the past year and showing a decline over the past 3 months. See our latest analysis for Synopsys. At a share price of US$432.65, Synopsys has seen a 1 day share price return of a 1.05% decline and a 90 day share price return of a 9.07% decline. Its 5 year total shareholder return of 84.85% contrasts with a much softer 0.46%...

headline: Synopsys eDT Platform Opens New Automotive Software Growth Question For Investors
summary: Synopsys (NasdaqGS:SNPS) has introduced its Electronics Digital Twin (eDT) Platform for automotive electronic systems. The eDT Platform focuses on cloud-based collaboration and early software validation for next generation, software defined vehicles. The launch expands Synopsys' offerings into digital twin technology tailored to the needs of automotive electronics developers. For you as an investor, this move increases attention on how Synopsys is positioning itself in automotive, a sector...

headline: Synopsys Unveils New Tools For Designing AI Chips, Systems
summary: Electronic design automation firm Synopsys unveiled new engineering solutions for developing AI chips and systems.

headline: Synopsys rolls out new software tools for designing AI chips
summary: Synopsys on Wednesday rolled out new software tools to handle the fast-increasing complexity of ‌designing artificial intelligence chips, the first wave of new offerings ‌after its $35 billion buyout of engineering software firm Ansys.  Synopsys, which announced the new tools ​at a conference in Silicon Valley, has for decades been one of the main suppliers of software used in determining how to arrange the tens of billions of transistors that make up chips from firms such ‌as Advanced Micro Devices ⁠and Nvidia, which last year invested in $2 billion Synopsys.

headline: Synopsys Introduces Software-Defined Hardware-Assisted Verification to Enable AI Proliferation
summary: Synopsys, Inc. (Nasdaq: SNPS) today announced advancements across its leading hardware-assisted verification (HAV) portfolio, including new hardware platforms and capabilities to support the ever-expanding demand for AI chip verification from the data center to the edge. Synopsys HAV platforms, powered by the company's unique software-defined capabilities, set new performance, scalability, and use case benchmarks for verifying the world's most sophisticated multi-die and AI chips amidst compound

headline: Synopsys Outlines Vision for Engineering the Future
summary: Today, Synopsys, Inc. (Nasdaq: SNPS) opened Synopsys Converge 2026, its new flagship conference, with a keynote by Synopsys president and CEO, Sassine Ghazi, who shared his vision for a new silicon-to-systems design paradigm for the era of pervasive intelligence — one that is silicon-powered, AI-enabled, and software-defined. He also announced new engineering solutions across Synopsys' expanded portfolio to advance how innovators design, verify, and deliver next generation AI-powered products.

headline: Synopsys Launches Ansys 2026 R1 to Re-Engineer Engineering with Joint Solutions and AI-Powered Products
summary: Synopsys, Inc. (NASDAQ: SNPS) today launched Ansys 2026 R1, delivering the first wave of integrated Synopsys-Ansys capabilities built on nearly a century of combined engineering expertise. The release also expands the Ansys simulation AI portfolio with a new, AI-enhanced training offering designed to make learning more effective and efficient, along with advanced AI features that help engineering teams gain earlier system‑level insight, reduce reliance on physical testing, and optimize performan
